SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.automation

Interface WriteValidator

All Known Implementing Classes:
DefaultWriteValidators.CompressorWriteValidator, DefaultWriteValidators.CoolerWriteValidator, DefaultWriteValidators.HeaterWriteValidator, DefaultWriteValidators.PumpWriteValidator, DefaultWriteValidators.SeparatorWriteValidator, DefaultWriteValidators.ThrottlingValveWriteValidator
public interface WriteValidator
Strategy interface for validating writes to a specific equipment class before they are applied to the simulation. Concrete implementations check that a proposed value is physically meaningful and consistent with the equipment's current state (for example: outlet pressure greater than inlet pressure for a compressor, efficiency in [0,1] for a pump, or opening percentage in [0,100] for a control valve).

Validators are registered in WriteValidatorRegistry and invoked by ProcessAutomation.setVariableValueValidated(String, double, String) and ProcessAutomation.setValuesTransactional(java.util.Map, String) before any write is applied. A WriteValidationResult with valid == false causes the entire transactional batch to be aborted and rolled back.

Implementations must be stateless and thread-safe; they may be invoked concurrently by multiple agents sharing the same registry.

Version:
1.0
Author:
Even Solbraa

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Class<? extends ProcessEquipmentInterface>
    getEquipmentClass()
    Returns the equipment class this validator applies to.
    WriteValidationResult
    validate(ProcessEquipmentInterface equipment, String propertyPath, double value, String unit)
    Validates a proposed write to the given equipment.

  • Method Details

    • getEquipmentClass

      Class<? extends ProcessEquipmentInterface> getEquipmentClass()
      Returns the equipment class this validator applies to. The registry uses Class.isAssignableFrom(Class) for dispatch, so a validator registered for a base class will also be invoked for its subclasses unless a more specific validator is registered.
      Returns:
      the equipment class this validator applies to; must not be null

    • validate

      WriteValidationResult validate(ProcessEquipmentInterface equipment, String propertyPath, double value, String unit)
      Validates a proposed write to the given equipment.
      Parameters:
      equipment - the target equipment instance; never null
      propertyPath - the local property path on the equipment (the portion of the address after the unit name, for example "outletPressure" or "feedStream.temperature"); never null
      value - the proposed numerical value
      unit - the unit of measure of value, or null for the property's default unit
      Returns:
      a non-null WriteValidationResult; use WriteValidationResult.ok() for a passing check
